item.title: SONiC Image Installation Guide
item.author: Noway@bimbimbambam (seisokeii)
item.description:
Every time I look at the AIS800-64O, it’s never fully populated… A full set of transceivers costs more than the switch itself.

Lately I’ve often met people who don’t know how to install SONiC. To save time sending the same instructions over and over, here’s a simple walkthrough.

First, you need to know about ONIE. I previously already wrote an ONIE introduction.

There are two main ways to install or upgrade SONiC:

item.title: SONiC Community Image Initial Setup
item.author: Noway@bimbimbambam (seisokeii)
item.description:
Above image is just for verifying some concepts then build a small Lab QQ

In EVE-NG or GNS3 simulator, running the SONiC KVM image will leave a lot of settings after booting. It’s annoying to pull out a bunch of settings every time you boot a new machine.

In this situation, I would like to automate the solution ( computer science people => lazy), but it seems that I don’t need to use Ansible, pyATS, or Nornir yet. So I use shell script to solve it.
